blocks.text: had given Davenport that. He was persuadable. That was enough for now.
had his doubts. Davenport was working from procedure. Julian was working from something else.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Audra Mills
blocks.content: Young, healthy male. No obvious external trauma. Could be cardiac. Could be a number of things. I won't know more until tox.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Audra Mills
blocks.content: Twelve to fifteen hours, roughly. Which puts it somewhere in the nine to midnight window.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: The group heard an argument at 9:15.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Audra Mills
blocks.content: Bagged it already. We'll run it.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Audra Mills
blocks.content: I'm thinking I don't think anything until I have results. You know better than to ask me that.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Audra Mills
blocks.content: Unofficially â€” the room was cold. Window open in forty-seven degree weather, no blanket pulled up. Either he went to sleep like that or someone opened the window after.
She'd seen the same thing I had. The water glass. The window. The way the room was arranged just slightly too deliberately.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Audra Mills
blocks.content: Rush it â€” seventy-two hours minimum.

blocks.prompt: Dr. Mills is careful. What does Julian read between her lines?

options.label: She suspects this isn't natural. She's just not saying it yet.
She's keeping an open mind. Professionally, that's the right call.
